_notes: HC2 (same-city home clash) demoted to SC7: historical data shows 14-34 clashes/season.
SC1/SC2 consecutive run thresholds raised from 3 to 5: historical mean is 7-8 games.
HC7 added: Christmas Day blackout (Dec 25) — firm British/PL convention.
HC8 added: Round 38 simultaneous kickoff — mandatory since 1994-95 (anti-collusion).
SC8 added: UEFA 5-day rest rule — Europa/Conference League Thursday → PL ≥5 days.
SC9 added: Easter coverage — all 20 teams play Good Friday AND Easter Monday.
SC10 added: London cluster cap — police/TfL cannot support >3 London home games/day.
SC11 added: Promoted team separation — 3 promoted clubs avoid each other in rounds 1-3.
SC12 added: Opening balance — no >3 consecutive H or A in rounds 1-5.
PR4 added: High-risk derby early kickoff preference (police deployment requirement).
PR5 added: Top-6 primetime distribution preference (broadcaster requirement).

hard.source: PL Handbook — clubs must have minimum 3 clear days between fixtures
hard.description: Each team must have at least 3 days between consecutive fixtures

hard.source: FIFA regulations — mandatory international break windows, clubs must release players
hard.description: No fixtures scheduled during FIFA-mandated international break windows
Every team plays every other team exactly twice (once home, once away)
A team can only appear in one fixture per slot
A ground can host at most one game per slot

hard.source: FA/PL calendar — no top-flight fixtures on Christmas Day, established convention
PL rule since 1994-95 — all Round 38 games kick off simultaneously to prevent collusion on title/relegation
hard.description: All 10 final-day fixtures must kick off at the same time on the same day

hard.source: Sky Sports Friday Night Football — broadcaster allocates ~10-15 Friday slots per season; no team plays more than 2-3 Friday games
hard.description: Each team plays at most 3 games on Friday across the full season

hard.source: PL scheduling practice — mid-week fixtures (Tue/Wed) occur only during designated double-gameweek windows; teams typically play 8-10 mid-week games per season
hard.description: Each team plays at most 10 games on Tuesday or Wednesday combined across the full season

hard.source: Sky Sports Monday Night Football — historical data (2015-2025) shows teams average 7-8 Monday appearances/season (league-wide 18-24% of fixtures on Monday)
hard.description: Each team plays at most 7 games on Monday across the full season

hard.source: PL scheduling practice — Wednesday mid-week rounds are concentrated windows; no team plays more than 5-6 Wednesday games per season
hard.description: Each team plays at most 6 games on Wednesday across the full season

hard.source: Historical EPL data (2015-2025) — Thursday fixtures average 1.6-4.2% of total (6-16 games/season); teams play at most 1-2 Thursday games; Thursday slots reserved primarily for NYD when it falls on Thursday
hard.description: Each team plays at most 2 games on Thursday across the full season

soft.source: PL scheduling preference — relaxed from 3 to 5 to match historical reality (mean 8.3 max/season)
soft.description: Teams should not have more than 5 consecutive away games

soft.source: PL scheduling preference — relaxed from 3 to 5 to match historical reality (mean 7.9 max/season)
soft.description: Teams should not have more than 5 consecutive home games

soft.source: PL scheduling convention — derbies spread across the season
soft.description: Derby fixtures should be at least 8 rounds apart (first vs second leg)

soft.source: PL scheduling courtesy — CL/EL Tuesday/Wednesday → PL Saturday is 3-4 days
soft.description: Teams with midweek European fixtures (Tue/Wed) should have at least 3 days before next PL game

soft.source: PL scheduling standard — teams should have roughly equal H/A per half-season
soft.description: Teams should have 9-10 home and 9-10 away games in each half of the season (tolerance ±2)

soft.source: PL/FA convention — avoids fixture congestion against same opponent
soft.description: A team should not face the same opponent in both a cup and league game within 4 weeks

soft.source: Police resource constraint (Atos/PL confirmed): rival city pairs (LIV+EVE, MCI+MUN, ARS+TOT, CHE+FUL etc.) cannot be home in the SAME MATCHDAY WEEKEND (Fri–Mon window, not just same day). Widened from same-day after research confirmed police plan by matchday round, not calendar date.
soft.description: Avoid same-city teams both at home within the same matchday weekend window (4-day radius)

soft.source: UEFA Regulation / PL accommodation — Europa League / Conference League Thursday games; clubs must have 5 clear days before next PL fixture if they played Thu night
soft.description: Teams playing Thursday Europa/Conference League must have ≥5 days before next PL fixture

soft.source: PL scheduling tradition — Easter weekend has always featured all 20 clubs across Good Friday and Easter Monday; raised from 20 to 50 to compete with SC7 in the objective
soft.description: All 20 teams should play on Good Friday AND Easter Monday (high-attendance bank holiday fixtures)

soft.source: Metropolitan Police / Transport for London capacity — simultaneous matches at multiple London grounds overwhelm Tube and policing
soft.description: No more than 3 London clubs should have home fixtures on the same day

soft.source: PL scheduling convention — newly promoted teams are kept apart in early rounds to ease their transition and spread interest
soft.description: The three promoted clubs should not face each other in the first 3 rounds of the season

soft.source: PL scheduling standard — teams should not start the season with a very heavy home or away run
soft.description: No team should have more than 3 consecutive home or away games in rounds 1-5

soft.source: Atos/PL Golden Rule (confirmed via Atos press releases and PL documentation): in any 5 consecutive fixtures the H/A split must be 3+2 or 2+3. This is the primary structural constraint the sequencing algorithm enforces.
soft.description: In any sliding window of 5 consecutive fixtures, a team must have exactly 2 or 3 home games (never 0, 1, 4, or 5)

soft.source: Atos/PL Golden Rule: teams must never start OR finish the season with 2 consecutive home or 2 consecutive away fixtures. Confirmed in multiple Atos and PL scheduling documentation.
soft.description: Teams cannot start the season with H+H or A+A, and cannot finish with H+H or A+A in their last two fixtures

soft.source: Atos/PL Golden Rule: if a team is at home on Boxing Day (Dec 26) they must be away on New Year's Day (Jan 1), and vice versa. Ensures equitable festive travel burden across all clubs.
soft.description: A team's Boxing Day and New Year's Day fixtures must have opposite H/A designation (home on one, away on the other)

soft.source: PL scheduling practice — the season is structured as 33 weekend + 5 midweek rounds (confirmed PL/Atos documentation). The 5 midweek rounds exist precisely to absorb postponed fixtures via double-gameweeks. Keeping ≥1 unfilled midweek date (Tue/Wed/Thu) per month ensures there is always a rescheduling window available without extending the season. Penalty is intentionally low (5) as this is a planning preference, not an operational requirement.
soft.description: Each calendar month should retain at least 1 unfilled midweek date (Tue/Wed/Thu with no league fixtures) as a contingency rescheduling window for postponed matches

preferences.source: Broadcaster requirement — Sky/TNT contracts specify marquee fixtures in peak slots
preferences.description: High-profile derbies should be placed in TV primetime slots

preferences.source: PL tradition — Boxing Day and New Year's Day are the highest-attended fixture windows
preferences.description: All 20 teams should have a fixture on Boxing Day, December 28, and New Year's Day

preferences.source: Broadcaster preference — opening fixture on Friday or Saturday evening maximises audience
preferences.description: Matchday 1 should include at least one high-profile fixture in an evening slot

preferences.source: Metropolitan Police / NPCC guidance — early kickoffs limit pre-match drinking time and allow police redeployment before evening matches
preferences.description: Designated high-risk (Category C) derbies should receive early kickoffs to assist police deployment

preferences.source: Sky Sports / TNT contract requirement — each top-6 club must appear in a minimum number of live broadcast slots
preferences.description: Each top-6 club should appear in at least 5 primetime broadcast slots (Sat 12:30, Sat 17:30, Sun 16:30)
